zh.lifeSummary: 當John de Burgh III於1414年6月12日出生在英國英格蘭格盧斯特斯爾可洛普頓時，他的父親Sir Hugh de Burgh35歲，母親Elizabeth de la Pole29歲。他於從1435年1月10日到1436年1月9日與Jana de Clopton在英國英格蘭格盧斯特斯爾可洛普頓結婚。他們至少是6個女兒的父母。他去世於1471年6月1日在英國林可爾史爾蓋斯波洛享年56歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当John de Burgh III于1414年6月12日出生在英国英格兰格盧斯特斯爾可洛普頓时，他的父亲Sir Hugh de Burgh35岁，母亲Elizabeth de la Pole29岁。他于从1435年1月10日到1436年1月9日与Jana de Clopton在英国英格兰格盧斯特斯爾可洛普頓结婚。他们至少是6个女儿的父母。他去世于1471年6月1日在英国林可爾史爾蓋斯波洛享年56岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Burgh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from one or other of the ten English places named Burgh in northern England and East Anglia, or the six named Brough in Derbyshire, Nottinghamshire, Westmorland, and Yorkshire. All these are from Old English burg, burh, Middle English burgh ‘massive earthwork, fortification; manor, borough’. Compare Bury . The placename Burgh is pronounced as borough. The surname Brough arose equally from places now called Burgh and Brough, and is the more common form of the surname in modern Britain, where it is pronounced bruff or broof (/brʊf/). The Scottish surname was probably brought in from Yorkshire. For the surname in Ireland see Burke .

variants.name: Burke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Kieran, Siobhan, Aileen, Brennan, Brigid, Donovan, Liam, Murphy, Ulick, Aidan, Dermot.

English and Irish (of Anglo-Norman origin): habitational name from Burgh (Castle) in Suffolk, England. Burk represents an Anglo-Norman pronunciation of Old English burg, Middle English burgh ‘fortification’ (see Burgh ).

variants.name: Bury
meanings: English (Lancashire): habitational name from Bury in Lancashire (now part of Greater Manchester), or from some other similarly named place. The placename comes from the dative case, byrig, of Old English burh ‘fortified place’.

Walloon and French: habitational name from any of several places so named, in Wallonia (Belgium) and Oise (France). The placename is from Buriacum, the name of a Gallo-Roman estate, composed of the personal name Burius + the locative suffix -acum. This surname is also found in the Flemish part of Belgium.

variants.name: Brough
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from any of various English places called Brough or Burgh. See   Burgh  .
Scottish: habitational name from Brough in Harray (Orkney).
Americanized form of German   Brauch   or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.

variants.name: Burrow
meanings: topographic name for someone who lived by a castle or manor house, or in a town, especially a chartered town or borough (Middle English burgh, Old English burg ‘fortified place, borough, manor’), or a habitational name for someone who came from a place so named because it was a fortified town or near an ancient fort, such as Burrow (Leicestershire, Lancashire), Burrough Green (Cambridgeshire), Brough (numerous counties), and Peterborough (Northamptonshire); see also Burgh .

topographic name for someone who lived on a hill or near a mound (Middle English bergh, Old English beorg), or a habitational name for someone who came from a place so named, such as Burrow (Devon, Somerset). Middle English bergh became throughly confused with burgh in 1 above. Compare Burrough .

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When John de Burgh III was born on 12 June 1414, in Clopton, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Sir Hugh de Burgh, was 35 and his mother, Elizabeth de la Pole, was 29. He married Jana de Clopton from 10 January 1435 to 9 January 1436, in Clopton, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 6 daughters. He died on 1 June 1471, in Gainsborough, Lincolnshire, England, at the age of 56.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque John de Burgh III est né le 12 juin 1414, à Clopton, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir Hugh de Burgh, avait 35 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth de la Pole, avait 29 ans. Il a épousé Jana de Clopton du 10 janvier 1435 au 9 janvier 1436, à Clopton, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 6 filles. Il est décédé le 1 juin 1471, à Gainsborough, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 56 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als John de Burgh III am 12. Juni 1414 in Clopton, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Hugh de Burgh 35 und seine Mutter Elizabeth de la Pole 29. Er hat von 10. Januar 1435 bis 9. Januar 1436 in Clopton, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom Jana de Clopton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Töchter. Er starb am 1. Juni 1471 im Alter von 56 Jahren in Gainsborough, Lincolnshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando John de Burgh III è nato il il 12 giugno 1414 nel Clopton, Gloucestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Sir Hugh de Burgh, aveva 35 anni e il suo madre, Elizabeth de la Pole, aveva 29 anni. Lui sposato Jana de Clopton da 10 gennaio 1435 a 9 gennaio 1436, nel Clopton, Gloucestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figlie. Morì il 1 giugno 1471, nel Gainsborough, Lincolnshire, England, all'età di 56 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: John de Burgh IIIが1414年6月12日にイギリスイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアクロプトンで生まれた時，父Sir Hugh de Burghは35歳，母Elizabeth de la Poleは29歳であった。から1435年1月10日まで1436年1月9日にイギリスイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアクロプトンでJana de Cloptonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の娘の親であった。彼は1471年6月1日にイギリスリンコルンシュアイアガインスボロウで56歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: John de Burgh III(이)가 1414년 6월 12일 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 클로프턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Hugh de Burgh(은)는 35세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth de la Pole(은)는 29세였다. 그는 1435년 1월 10일부터 1436년 1월 9일까지 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 클로프턴에서 Jana de Clopton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1471년 6월 1일 영국 링코실르 게인스버루그h에서 56세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando John de Burgh III nasceu em 12 de junho de 1414, em Clopton, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Sir Hugh de Burgh, tinha 35 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth de la Pole, tinha 29 anos. Ele casou-se com Jana de Clopton de 10 de janeiro de 1435 a 9 de janeiro de 1436, em Clopton, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhas. Ele faleceu em 1 de junho de 1471, em Gainsborough, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, com 56 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда John de Burgh III родился 12 июня 1414 г. в местности клоптон, глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Sir Hugh de Burgh было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth de la Pole, 29. Он женился на женщине по имени Jana de Clopton с 10 января 1435 по 9 января 1436 в местности клоптон, глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 6 дочерей. Он умер 1 июня 1471 г. в местности геинсбороую, линколншир, Англия в возрасте 56 лет.
